content.plain_english: This week in Year 1 maths, your child is working on Length and height. The learning focuses on: compare, describe and solve practical problems for:; lengths and heights; and mass / weight. These skills build directly on what they have already mastered and prepare them for the next steps in the programme of study.
content.in_school: Children use practical measuring equipment — rulers, scales, measuring cylinders, and clocks — to explore measurement in real contexts. They record findings using diagrams and number lines before progressing to written calculations. Problem-solving tasks show how these skills are used every day.
content.home_activity: Use a piece of string to measure things around the house — is the sofa longer or shorter than the table? Estimate in centimetres first, then measure to check. For perimeter, measure all four sides of a book or tile and add them up.
dinner_table_questions: Can you show me how to compare, describe and solve practical problems for:?
Was it easy or tricky to lengths and heights? Why?
How would you explain today's maths to someone who has never seen it before?

key_vocabulary.definition: How long something is from one end to the other.
A very small unit of length — 10 mm = 1 cm.
A unit of length — 100 cm = 1 metre.
A unit of length — 1000 m = 1 kilometre.
Make a sensible guess at a measurement before you measure it.

content.times_table_tip: Year 1 focuses on the 2, 5, and 10 times tables. Try counting in 2s together on the stairs, or in 10s while counting out coins. Even 2–3 minutes a day builds the familiarity that pays off in Year 2.
